_description: Patterns commonly considered sensitive PII under US jurisdictions (notably CCPA).
_disclaimer: Using this profile does not constitute compliance with CCPA, HIPAA, or any framework. It only enables redaction of patterns commonly considered sensitive in the named jurisdiction.

patterns.name: us-ssn
patterns.notes: US SSN with mandatory hyphen separators (NNN-NN-NNNN). Hyphen-less 9-digit SSNs are not matched; redact those via the standard env-style patterns or the aggressive level.

patterns.name: us-ein
patterns.notes: US Employer Identification Number (NN-NNNNNNN). Requires 'EIN' prefix; the bare NN-NNNNNNN shape collides with too many other ID conventions.

patterns.name: us-routing
patterns.notes: US bank routing number is 9 digits with an ABA checksum, but 9-digit substrings appear constantly (zip+4, ints, ids). Requiring 'routing' or 'ABA' prefix is the safe narrowing for v0.5.0.
